data.title: Boeing Surprise: More Airplane Deliveries Compared To Airbus
data.description:
In January, I discussed the net orders and deliveries for Boeing (NYSE:BA) and Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) in 2025. Based on reader feedback, I am bringing the series back on a monthly basis. The aim of the series is to assess the recovery of Boeing relative to Airbus. This allows us to provide context on how much of the recovery is driven by improvements at Boeing. Secondly, it allows us to assess how much of the recovery is attributable to improvements of the aerospace supply chain.

Explanation Of Analysis Methodology

For this analysis, I will be using an internally developed tool available on The Aerospace Forum. I will be assessing the net orders and deliveries. For each, I will discuss the units ordered and delivered and the associated dollar value. The net orders provide us with a view of demand. This is a reflection of factors such as pricing and availability. Boeing and Airbus have a total backlog of nearly 15,000 airplanes. So, currently we are not viewing things through a scope of needing more orders to support or increase production. The fact is that the backlogs have to be brought down to healthier levels and that can be achieved through higher deliveries. Deliveries is what matters and that is also the area where we see that Boeing nor Airbus have been able to support demand. Assessing the deliveries gives us a view on how the recovery for both manufacturers is tracking.

Airbus Books More Airplane In Orders Than Boeing In January

The table above shows the net orders by units as well as dollar value for both jet makers. I have also included a column marking the “winner”, but I do note that a single month does not provide a trend indicator. While I have a winner column in the table, I am not so much interested to view things through a lens of who is winning and who is losing. At least not at this point.

The table indicates that Boeing booked more single aisle orders in January while Airbus booked more wide body orders. In total, Airbus booked 15 more orders than Boeing in January and that also translated to the dollar value which was $3.4 billion higher.

Airbus And Boeing Book $7.6 Billion In Airplane Orders

Boeing and Airbus logged a total of 87 airplane orders in January. Airbus took a 59% share in the orders with the remaining 41% going to Boeing. Year-on-year, the net orders increased by 56 airplanes with both manufacturers logging more orders. In terms of value, the increase was $2.6 billion or 52%. The increase was driven by Boeing, which logged a net increase of $2.2 billion in order value. What I do note is that the order value stood at a multi-year high. Only in January 2020, the order value was higher. Driven by a more favorable mix, Airbus took 72% of the order value leaving 28% for Boeing.

Boeing Delivers More Airplanes In January

The reason why I take a data driven approach on things is because it provides a measurable basis. Currently, the perception is that Airbus is doing much better than Boeing. However, for January the figures show otherwise. This of course just one month of deliveries, which provides no indication of what the full year be like.

Boeing deliveries were higher than usual due to the fact that driven by the timing of the employee strike and work resumption, it could not deliver all airplanes in inventory in December. Usually, we see a bump in deliveries in December and a weaker January delivery volume due to this phenomenon.

Airbus, on the other hand, is facing a shortage of engines which will keep airplane deliveries lower in the first quarter of the year. The European jet maker is balancing getting grounded P&W powered airplanes back in the air while also having a flow of engine deliveries to support new engine deliveries. Furthermore, Safran (OTCPK:SAFRF) which provides the CFM LEAP 1A turbofan is recovering from production issues including hurricane damage. So, besides providing the data we also need to put things in context. The context shows different dynamics for both jet makers.

Driven by the higher delivery volumes, Boeing also saw its delivery surpass that of Airbus by $1.3 billion. This brought the delivery value to $2.8 billion for Boeing and $1.5 billion for Airbus.

Airbus And Boeing Book $7.6 Billion In Airplane Orders

Boeing and Airbus logged a total of 70 airplane orders in January. Airbus took a 36% share in the orders with the remaining 64% going to Boeing. Year-on-year, the net orders increased by 13 airplanes. Boeing delivered saw delivery volumes grow 18 units booking a multi-year high for January delivery numbers. Airbus deliveries decreased by five units. The delivery numbers show that Airbus has been alternating higher and lower deliveries over the past years and January 2025 was again a low year.

The increase in dollar value of deliveries was $1.1 billion or 33%. The increase was driven by Boeing, which logged a net increase of $1.2 billion. Airbus delivery value partially offset the total growth in delivery value. Boeing accounted for 65% of the value. The remaining 35% went to Airbus. Delivery value for Boeing was at the highest point since 2019. Total delivery value was at the highest point since 2022. Combined delivery value is still 28% below the highs of 2019. This shows a significant upward potential on delivery rates. Boeing only lagged $0.7 billion compared to 2019. Airbus was $1.1 billion behind. It shows that challenges on deliveries persist on both sides.

Conclusion: A Better Year Expected For Both Jet Makers

Boeing and Airbus saw higher net order inflow in January. Currently, I am not interested in the number of net orders. Both jet makers have backlogs of thousands of airplanes. What matters at this point is the number of deliveries. Airplane deliveries stood at a multi-year high. Timing of Boeing’s airplane deliveries were the reason for the increase in deliveries. The increase was partially offset by lower deliveries for Airbus.

The delivery flow in Q1 will not be smooth. However, as we progress throughout the year, we should be able to see robust growth. Continuous assessment of the deliveries provides a sense on where both jet makers stand in their attempt to increase production.

I mark both stocks a buy. Boeing is on a trajectory to improve production quality and output. Airbus, on the other hand, is aligning its supply chain with its production capacity. Both companies are facing different challenges, but the overall picture is a positive one for higher output.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: PFFV: Preferred Shares Index ETF, Above-Average Yield And Performance Track-Record
data.description:
The Global X Variable Rate Preferred ETF (NYSEARCA:PFFV) invests in variable rate preferred shares, sports an above-average 7.2% dividend yield, below-average credit risk and duration. It is a strong investment opportunity, and a buy.

PFFV - Overview and Analysis

Index and Portfolio

PFFV invests in variable rate preferred shares. Preferreds are something of a hybrid asset class, with characteristics of both equities and bonds. In PFFV's case, these investments behave much more similarly to bonds than to equities, with above-average dividends and little possibility of substantial capital appreciation, under most conditions at least.

PFFV itself is an index ETF, tracking the ICE U.S. Variable Rate preferred Securities Index. Said index includes most variable rate preferreds subject to a basic set of inclusion criteria. It is a market-cap weighted index, with issuer caps meant to prevent excessive concentration.

The fund itself is much less diversified than average, with only 53 different investments, almost entirely banks. Banks tend to issue preferreds much more so than average, partly due to greater financial acumen, but mostly due to favorable (or less unfavorable) regulations. Banks need to meet certain capital requirements, tightened since the financial crisis, and preferreds help them meet these, hence their overweight position in PFFV and similar funds.

PFFV's overweight financials position increases risk, volatility, and exposes investors to significant losses and underperformance due to industry weakness. This has yet to be an issue for PFFV, but it has been an issue for some of its older peers. As an example, the iShares Preferred and Income Securities ETF (PFF), the largest ETF in this space, saw +60% drawdowns during the financial crisis, vastly exceeding high-yield bond losses. The financial crisis was concentrated in the banking sector, hence the fund's significant losses.

Although another recession centered on the banking industry seems unlikely, if only because we have no reason to expect one, it is definitely possible, and a risk for PFFV. More diversified funds don't suffer from these issues. Due to this, I wouldn't overweight PFFV in my portfolio or use it as a core portfolio holding, to reduce risk.

Rate Risk

PFFV invests in variable rate preferreds, which function a bit differently than other variable rate investments. These preferreds are generally fixed-to-variable, paying a fixed rate for an initial period of time, variable rate thereafter. As an example, one of PFFV's investment is a Bank of America preferred issued in 2015, with a 6.1% fixed rate until 2025, variable rate of 3m SOFR plus 4.15% thereafter. Specific preferred is as follows, you might have to squint a bit to read the table:

As should be clear from the example above, some of these variable preferreds have fixed rates for a very long amount of time, so, in practice, behave as fixed-rate securities. PFFV does not provide specific figures and, due to the complexities of these securities, it is not possible for me to estimate these either. Still, PFFV has some rate risk, even though it is nominally a variable rate fund.

PFFV saw losses of 13.8% during 2022, when the Fed started to hike rates, in-line with broader bond index ETFs / other bond sub-asset classes. Duration thus seems about average, of around 5 - 7 years.

PFFV's average duration does not significantly alter the fund's investment thesis, or lack thereof, nor is it an important advantage or disadvantage relative to peers. It is an important, and surprising, consideration for investors though. Dovish investors might prefer funds with higher duration, hawkish / risk-averse in those with zero. Lots will be surprised about a variable rate ETF having a moderate amount of rate risk, instead of close to zero.

Credit Risk

PFFV focuses on BBB-BB investments, basically in the middle of the pack.

Credit quality and risk are both moderate, higher than that of investment-grade funds, lower than that of high-yield funds. With inception in mid-2020, PFFV has yet to experience a downturn or recession, so we can't really gauge its performance during one. I would expect some losses during one, although far from significant ones.

Overall Volatility

PFFV's moderate rate and credit risk should result in moderate volatility, as seems to be the case. There are periods of above-average volatility though, including during most of 2025.

Dividend Yield Analysis

PFFV sports a 7.2% dividend yield, moderately higher than that of most bonds, slightly higher than most high-yield bonds.

It is a good yield on an absolute basis, and quite good for a fund with PFFV's credit risk. Remember, the fund invests quite heavily in BBB preferreds, and very little in those below BB. Quality is moderately higher than the average high-yield bond ETF, dividend yields marginally higher.

Most variable rate ETFs have seen their dividend yields increase almost in lock-step with Fed rates and so sport above-average yields because Fed rates have risen. I'm not sure that this is the case for PFFV, as most of the fund's preferreds are fixed-to-variable. Comparing the fund's dividend yield with that of the Janus Henderson AAA CLO ETF (JAAA) shows that PFFV's yield has risen by less than expected for a variable rate ETF too.

In my opinion, and considering the above, PFFV's strong, above-average yield is not entirely due to Fed hikes and so should prove somewhat resilient in the face of cuts. In the very long-term cuts should lead to lower dividend yields, but it could take over a decade for these to fully materialize.

In any case, PFFV's above-average yield is an important benefit for shareholders, especially considering the fund's risk profile. Income is above-average, risk just about average, a good combination.

Performance Track-Record

PFFV's performance track-record is reasonably good, with the fund outperforming most bonds and bond sub-asset classes since inception, matching the performance of high-yield corporate bonds. Relative to these, the fund outperformed prior to 2022, underperformed since. Some combination of greater exposure to rates / spreads seems likely to explain these results, although I'm not confident in this. Returns seem effectively identical since around 2023.

PFFV's performance track-record should have been materially stronger if the fund had experienced the entirety of the pandemic and had seen lower losses than high-yield bonds during it (consistent with credit ratings). In that case, we would have a fund with comparable returns to high-yield bonds, slightly higher yields, slightly lower drawdowns. It would be a potent combination, if not an outstanding one.

We don't have the data though, which is an issue here. For me, the credit quality data we do have is more than enough, for others perhaps not. The performance information we do have is reasonably good too, regardless.

Conclusion

PFFV's above-average 7.2% dividend yield, below-average credit risk and reasonably good performance track-record make it a buy.

data.title: Texas Instruments: Investors Should Be Cautious As The Valuation Is Detached From Reality
data.description:
Thesis

Despite the company's struggles, Texas Instruments Inc. (NASDAQ:TXN) stock has gained over 25% in the past year. In the below analysis I find that the technicals are very strong currently but the fundamentals show a different story. While Q4 still had negative revenue and EPS growth, there was significant improvement over mid 2024 results. However, guidance shows that the recovery is on hold as the declines in these financials are expected to reaccelerate in Q1. While that is the case, both the P/S and P/E ratios near three year highs leading me to believe that the stock is materially overvalued currently. Therefore, since the technicals and the fundamentals clash on the stock's outlook, I believe a hold rating would be most appropriate.

Technicals

Charting

Trends

TXN stock was in a sharp downtrend from early November to late December but the stock broke out. Even though some declines still occurred after the breakout, the rapid declines seen in the downtrend seem to be over. In recent days, the stock has soared with multiple gaps, confirming the end of the downtrend. The increase in volume in recent days helps to show the validity of this surge. It is too early to conclude that TXN is now in an uptrend but nonetheless, the stock is in a good position.

Support

The former downtrend line could have acted as support but it has moved far out of range. Currently, the nearest source of support is at around the 200 level. This level acted as support for periods in October and November while being key resistance in January. The next support level is an upside gap at around 196. Even though the doji are quite close together, the fact that this level was major support in September and November, and that the volume on the day of the gap was very high, its importance is not to be doubted. Lastly, there is support at 186 as there was another upside gap there. The volume on that day was high as well and this level was support at the beginning of January.

Resistance

There is relatively little resistance for TXN stock in this timeframe. The only major resistance would be at around 211. This level was major resistance in late September and late October and so if the stock continues to rally, this area could potentially act as a ceiling.

Takeaway

I would argue that the chart is looking bullish for TXN. The stock broke out of the sharp downtrend and has recently surged. There is also much more support below the stock than there is resistance above and so investors should find this price action to be encouraging.

Moving Averages

Simple Moving Average ('SMA')

The 50 day SMA had a death cross with the 200 day SMA back in January. The gap between the SMAs expanded to a great degree, showing accelerating long term bearish momentum. However, in recent days the 50 day SMA's trajectory has turned positive and so bearishness may be starting to recede. With the stock now above both SMAs, expect the gap between the lines to close.

Exponential Moving Average ('EMA')

The 9 day EMA just had a bullish crossover with the 21 day EMA, showing that there has been near term strength in TXN stock. Given the extremely sharp trajectory of the 9 day EMA, expect the gap between these EMAs to expand rapidly to reflect the quickly accelerating near term bullish momentum.

Bollinger Bands

In recent days, the stock soared above the Bollinger Bands' midline as a bullish signal. This helped to provide confirmation that another downtrend is not forming. Instead, this breakout could foreshadow that the stock is entering into a new uptrend. Currently, the stock is far above the upper band and so it is likely significantly overbought. Therefore an overbought correction is likely in the near future.

Takeaway

I believe the moving averages as a whole are quite bullish for TXN stock. The SMAs are still in an overall bearish position but they show that longer term bearishness could be starting to recede. The EMAs reflect the rapidly increasing near term bullishness and lastly, the stock's breakout above the Bollinger Bands' midline is a significant signal with multiple positive implications.

Indicators

Moving Average Convergence Divergence ('MACD')

The MACD just had a bullish crossover with its signal line in recent days. The MACD line has soared and so the gap between the lines has expanded rapidly, as demonstrated by the very strong histogram. This indicates that bullish momentum is soaring.

Relative Strength Index ('RSI')

The RSI is currently at 69.39, very close to entering the 70+ zone. This indicator has soared past the 50 level in recent days showing that there has been a major change in sentiment as the bulls are now in solid control of the stock. The RSI is currently at six month highs despite the stock being far below its six month high, showing that there is extraordinary strength building in TXN stock.

Stochastics

From late January to mid February, there were multiple crossovers in the stochastics that ended up being false signals with both lines remaining in the 20 zone. A true bullish crossover occurred recently as the %K pulled away from the %D. Both lines have soared past the 50 level and the %K has entered into the 80+ zone, further evidence that the bulls are now in firm control.

Takeaway

In my view, there is no doubt that these indicators are bullish for TXN stock. The MACD just had a bullish crossover and shows accelerating momentum, the RSI is at its highest level in six months, and the stochastics displayed many promising indications as of late.

Fundamentals

Earnings

Quarterly Results

TXN released their 2024 Q4 earnings in late January and showed relatively respectable results overall. They reported revenues of $4.01 billion, down 2% YoY and an EPS of $1.30, down 13% YoY. As you can see in the above chart, while revenue growth is negative, it represents great improvement since mid 2024 and is actually at around average levels in relation to the past three years. It is a similar story for EPS growth as its current negative growth still represents strong improvement from mid 2024 levels. EPS growth remains at below average levels compared to the past few years. Both revenue and GAAP EPS also showed strong performance versus consensus estimates as they beat by $134.57 million and $0.10 respectively. A highlight in their earnings would be free cash flow growth. They reported $1.498 billion FCF in their Q4 versus $1.349 billion in the prior year period, showing that growth was 11%. Overall, Q4 results show some recovery in the business, as there were significant improvements in key metrics.

Guidance

TXN provided guidance for their 2025 Q1. They are expecting revenues to range from $3.74 billion to $4.06 billion. This implies growth of between -6.7% and +1.2% and so at the midpoint, it would be a worsening of YoY revenue declines compared to Q4. They also provided EPS guidance of between $0.94 and $1.16. Therefore, EPS declines are expected to be in range of 10.8% to 27.7%. At the midpoint, this would show that YoY EPS declines are expected to worsen as well. After a promising Q4 quarter, I believe this guidance dents confidence as the recovery in TXN may be on hold according to these projections.

Valuation

Price To Sales Ratio (P/S)

The P/S ratio is currently at 11.98 and is at far above average levels compared to the past three years. I believe this ratio shows overvaluation in TXN stock. As discussed above, revenue growth has recovered significantly and is at average levels relative to the past few years. However, guidance shows that this recovery trend will not continue in Q1 as they expect declines in revenue to worsen modestly. These financial results and projections are unable to justify a P/S ratio that is far above average and near three year highs. This shows there is a significant disconnect between the valuation and fundamentals. Currently, the IT sector's median P/S ratio is only 3.32 and so TXN is at an over 260% premium to peers. Its negative growth cannot justify this rich of a premium to the sector in my view.

Price To Earnings Ratio (P/E)

It is a very similar story for the P/E ratio. It is currently at 39.3, far above the three year average and is in fact very near three year highs as well. I also believe it shows overvaluation in TXN stock. As discussed above, while EPS declines have moderated in Q4, overall growth is still below average. In addition, Q1 guidance shows that the declines are expected to worsen again. While this is the case, the P/E ratio nears multi year highs. This is a major disconnect that shows the valuation is detached from the financial results of the company. The IT sector's median GAAP P/E ratio is currently 31.71 and so the stock is also at a premium to peers according to this measure.

Takeaway

I conclude that TXN is significantly overvalued at current levels. Both the P/S and P/E ratios show significant disconnect with their respective financial growth figures. Both ratios are also at a premium to the sector while guidance shows that declines in revenue and EPS are expected to reaccelerate.

Conclusion

In the above analysis, I have determined that the technicals are very strong for TXN stock. However, the fundamentals show a different story. As discussed above, while Q4 results were respectable and showed improvement, guidance was weak and shows a re-acceleration in the declines in revenue and EPS. Furthermore, the valuation is currently detached from reality as the near multiyear highs in the P/S and P/E ratios cannot be justified by the state of the financials. Therefore, the technical analysis and fundamental analysis are highly split on their outlook for TXN stock and as a result I initiate TXN at a hold rating.
